name: TitleMax Title Pawns
description_short: TitleMax Title Pawns East Point, Georgia — TitleMax offers vehicle title pawns and loans in 14 states, providing fast cash against a car or motorcycle t...
description_long:
TitleMax was founded September 1, 1998, with its first store in Columbus, Georgia, and is headquartered at 15 Bull Street in Savannah. It operates under parent company TMX Finance LLC, a privately held enterprise owned by Savannah entrepreneur Tracy Young. TitleMax is strictly a for-profit, high-cost consumer lender — it holds no CDFI designation, HUD approval, or NFCC certification. Operating across 14 states with 900+ locations concentrated in the Southeast and South, the company filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy in April 2009 and emerged a year later. Since then it has remained under regulatory scrutiny, including two separate CFPB enforcement actions.

TitleMax's primary products are vehicle title loans and title pawns — short-term, high-interest advances secured by a borrower's car or motorcycle title. In Georgia and Alabama, these transactions are structured as title pawns under state pawn shop statutes rather than lending law, which grants the company significantly broader repossession rights than conventional loan agreements. Borrowers keep driving their vehicle during the term. Rates are steep: Georgia title pawns can reach 25% per month (300% theoretical APR), and typical annualized contract rates have been reported between 119% and 179%. Tennessee licenses allow up to 264% APR. In select states, TitleMax also offers personal installment loans requiring no vehicle. No credit check or bank account is needed to qualify, and funds can be disbursed in as little as 30 minutes.

With 900+ physical storefronts, TitleMax is one of the largest title lenders in the United States. Its scale translates into broad geographic access for unbanked and underbanked consumers who have few other options. The company also offers a mobile app for iOS and Android and an online customer portal at account.titlemax.com where borrowers can view balances, track payment history, and make payments by debit or credit card — features not universal among pawn-style lenders. Motorcycle title pawns are available in addition to auto loans, which distinguishes TitleMax from many competitors.

TitleMax addresses a real need — fast, collateral-based cash for consumers shut out of traditional credit — but the cost is among the highest in consumer finance. The CFPB fined TMX Finance $9 million in 2016 for deceptive practices and issued an additional $15 million settlement in 2023 for violating the Military Lending Act by charging active-duty servicemembers above the legally mandated 36% MAPR cap, as well as Truth in Lending Act violations and fees for non-file insurance that provided borrowers no actual benefit. TitleMax currently operates under a CFPB consent order. For Georgia borrowers specifically, the title pawn legal structure accelerates repossession timelines compared to standard loan law. Borrowers who cannot repay quickly risk losing their vehicle and may face a debt cycle through high-cost rollovers. TitleMax should be treated as a last-resort emergency option.

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee. Borrowers may repay early subject to state-specific prepayment rules. No refund or cancellation policy applies to title lending or title pawn transactions.

pros: One of the largest title lenders in the US with 900+ store locations across 14 states
No credit check or bank account required to qualify — accessible to unbanked consumers
Funds can be disbursed in as little as 30 minutes after approval
Borrower keeps driving their vehicle throughout the loan term
Offers motorcycle title pawns in addition to car title loans — less common among competitors
Mobile app (iOS and Android) and online portal at account.titlemax.com for payments and balance checks
cons: Georgia/Alabama title pawns can charge up to 25% per month, with typical APRs reported between 119% and 179% annually
CFPB fined TitleMax $15 million in 2023 for violating the Military Lending Act, TILA, and charging servicemembers above the 36% MAPR legal cap
Georgia title pawn structure under pawn shop law gives lender broader repossession rights than standard loan agreements, with faster timelines
Company filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy in 2009 and operates under a CFPB consent order requiring outside compliance oversight
No universal rate table published — pricing is opaque and varies significantly by state, loan amount, and vehicle value
best_for: Consumers with poor or no credit history who own a paid-off or nearly paid-off vehicle and need emergency cash
Unbanked individuals who cannot qualify for a traditional bank loan or credit card cash advance
Borrowers who need funds the same day and have exhausted lower-cost alternatives
diagnosis: TitleMax is best suited for unbanked or credit-damaged consumers who own a vehicle outright and face a genuine short-term cash emergency with no other viable options. The main caveat is cost: APRs routinely exceed 100% annually, Georgia title pawns carry aggressive repossession terms under pawn law rather than lending law, and the company has twice faced major CFPB enforcement actions for deceptive and illegal lending practices.
services: Car title pawns (Georgia and Alabama — structured under pawn shop statutes)
Personal installment loans (Arizona, Delaware, Kansas, Mississippi, Missouri, Nevada, South Carolina, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, Wisconsin)
Mobile app for iOS and Android (balance checks, payment history, debit/credit card payments)
Store locator and in-person customer service at 900+ locations
